description_long: This trash bin features a tall, rectangular body with a slightly tapered lower section and a flat, overhanging lid. The lid appears to be removable, sitting flush atop the main body with a subtle seam visible around its perimeter. The surface is smooth and uniform, with a matte gray finish that suggests it is made from metal or heavy-duty plastic. There are no visible handles, wheels, or external features, giving it a minimalist and utilitarian appearance. The bin is unmarked, lacking any signage, labels, or recycling symbols. Its design is typical for public waste receptacles found on city sidewalks, in parks, or near building entrances.
description: A tall, rectangular trash bin with a flat, removable lid and a smooth, matte gray finish. The design is minimalist, with no visible handles, wheels, or markings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the flat lid overhanging the main body, with a clear seam and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the same overhanging lid and tapered lower section, maintaining the bin's smooth, unadorned appearance.
description_view_2: Rear view is nearly identical to the front, with the flat lid and seamless body, lacking any distinguishing features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view matches the other side, showing the uniform, minimalist design and the absence of handles or wheels.
